evidence.scientific_clarity: Scatter + OLS line + R² annotation + highlighted OursModel point all present per brief; claim is legible, though log-axis nature only implied by axis labels.
evidence.typography: PDF uses Type3 (outlined) DejaVu Sans glyphs — the matplotlib default; script has no pdf.fonttype=42 setting, so text is not selectable/editable TrueType.
evidence.palette: Blue (#4878CF) scatter, red (#D65F5F) OLS line, orange (#E8A838) highlight — avoids pure red/green clash; reasonably colorblind-safe for a sequential/directional context.
evidence.layout_density: Panel space is well utilized with no large empty regions; legend and stats annotation box both occupy upper-right, slightly crowded but not overlapping.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure precisely; legend placed near the stats annotation box creating a crowded upper-right corner — Reviewer 2 would likely request separation.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF embeds Type3 fonts (Subtype /Type3 in PDF source), not Type42/TrueType; text glyphs are outlined shapes, not selectable or editable vectors.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (21 KB), figure.png (64 KB), caption.tex (390 B), gen_figure.py (2 KB); SVG also produced as bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states n=80, gives full OLS equation (slope −0.0449, intercept 0.6209), R²=0.877, and exact OursModel coordinates (9.449, 0.210) — strong numerical claim that matches the figure.

overall_strengths: Caption is exemplary: quantitative, precise, and fully aligned with what is shown in the figure.
All required output files are present and non-trivial, including a bonus SVG.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 setting: the PDF embeds Type3 (outlined) DejaVu Sans — the matplotlib default — making text non-selectable and non-editable.
Legend and stats annotation box compete for the same upper-right corner; repositioning one would improve readability and reviewer reception.
